Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Les K. Wright papers and Bear History Project files, 1959-2010.
Collection Number: 7656

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Les K. Wright papers and Bear History Project files, 1959-2010.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
7656
Abstract:
Documentation of a gay subculture revolving around burly, hairy men, or "bears." Includes newsletters, flyers, and correspondence from bear clubs, printouts from related electronic discussion lists, and material related to the writing and publication of THE BEAR BOOK, which Wright edited. Also, Wright's journals, personal memorabilia, and correspondence, including extensive correspondence with friend Billie Aul. Collection also includes many videorecordings relating to bears.
Creator:
Wright, Les K.
Aul, Billie.
Quanitities:
36.4 cubic feet.
4 volumes.
Language:
Collection material in English
RELATED MATERIALS

Collecting program: Human Sexuality Collection.

Young and hairy. By David Butt. (1999)
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Les K. Wright papers and Bear History Project files, #7656. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical

Leslie Kirk Wright edited THE BEAR BOOK (1997) and THE BEAR BOOK II (2001), curated Bear Icons art exhibitions in various cities, and promoted the preservation of bear culture through the Bear History Project and Nashoba Institute.
Scope and content

The Les K. Wright papers and Bear History Project files consists of documentation of a gay subculture revolving around burly, hairy men, or "bears", including newsletters, flyers, 4 scrapbooks and correspondence from bear clubs, printouts from related electronic discussion lists, and material related to the writing and publication of THE BEAR BOOK, which Wright edited, and files regarding his work on various bear events, the BEARSKIN BEND LODGE, BEAR HISTORY PROJECT NEWSLETTER, and the Nashoba Institute. The collection also includes, Leslie Kirk Wright's journals, personal memorabilia, and correspondence, including extensive correspondence with friend Billie Aul and a video of his 2002 civil union ceremony with Dale Robert Wehrle. Also included is much of Wright's academic work, notably his dissertation and tenure manuscripts, class syllabi, and other published and unpublished writings as well as documentation of his studies of folk music and artists in society at the German university, Universitat Tubingen in 1975-77. Diplomas and recognition of his teaching and academic career. Three scrapbooks from his time living in Central New York State, 2013 through August 2018. A Bear flag from "Bear Your Soul 2014 @ Easton Mountain" signed with expressions of gratitude to Les Wright for his leadership in the Bear community. The collection also includes many bear-related videorecordings..
SUBJECTS

Names:
Nashoba Institute
Subjects:
Bears (Gay culture)
Body image.
Masculinity.
Group identity.
Gender identity
Gay men -- Attitudes.
Gay people -- Identity
